description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves held together by three wide, dark metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and grayish, suggesting exposure to the elements or age, with visible grain and texture on each stave. The top and bottom are flat and made from the same wood, with the top surface slightly inset and bordered by a raised rim. The metal bands are evenly spaced, providing structural reinforcement and a visual contrast to the lighter wood. There are no visible handles, spouts, or markings on the exterior, giving it a utilitarian and rustic appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, courtyards, or near storage areas, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with three dark metal hoops, flat top and bottom, and no visible handles or markings. The wood is grayish and textured, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the barrel with all three metal hoops and the flat wooden top clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing the curvature of the staves and the texture of the wood, with the metal hoops prominent.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the symmetry of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view similar to the front, with the same features visible and no additional details or markings.
